content.verdict: Lizard Island is the only luxury private-island resort directly on the northern Great Barrier Reef — 1,000 acres, 24 white-sand beaches, 40 suites, and access to the legendary Cod Hole dive site. Fly-in only (1h from Cairns). Fully all-inclusive (meals, premium drinks, snorkel gear, motorised dinghy). The closest mainstream comparison is a Maldives private-island resort, only the snorkeling is arguably better.
content.best_room: The Pavilion (1 unit, $4,000+/night) is the original honeymoon suite — total privacy, plunge pool, ocean views. The Sunset Point Villas have direct beach access and the best swimming-distance reef. Anchor Bay Suites are the value-luxury entry with full ocean view from bed.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival via Cairns
itinerary_7_nights.description: Fly into Cairns (CNS), transfer to Lizard Island via 1h East Air charter. Welcome on the dock with champagne. Beach picnic for lunch. Afternoon orientation snorkel from your villa beach. Dinner at Saltwater Restaurant — kingfish ceviche, Bowen mango.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Cod Hole half-day dive
itinerary_7_nights.description: Half-day boat trip to the legendary Cod Hole — encounter giant potato cod up to 100kg in shallow water. Even snorkellers see them clearly. Return for lunch on Sunset Point Beach. Spa massage in the afternoon. Sundowners on the dive boat dock.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Private dinghy & secluded beach picnic
itinerary_7_nights.description: Take your motorised dinghy (included) to one of the 24 beaches. Crew drops a chilled hamper at Mermaid Cove or Watson's Bay. Snorkel, swim, nap. Return at sunset. Dinner under the stars on Anchor Bay beach.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Cook's Look hike + reef snorkel
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning hike up Cook's Look (358m, 3h round-trip) — the same vantage Captain Cook climbed in 1770 to find a passage through the reef. Spectacular 360° view. Afternoon snorkel at Clam Garden. Dinner of grilled coral trout.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Outer reef expedition
itinerary_7_nights.description: Full-day trip to the outer Great Barrier Reef — typically Hicks Reef or Day Reef. Three snorkel/dive sites, lunch on the boat. Reef visibility on a good day is 30m+. Return at sunset for a quiet dinner in your villa.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Spa + clam garden snorkel
itinerary_7_nights.description: Slow morning, breakfast on your terrace. Couples' Essence Spa Ritual (90 min) at the spa pavilion. Afternoon snorkel at the resort's signature Clam Garden — giant clams up to 1.2m. Sundown cocktails on Sunset Beach.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell breakfast & departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Final reef snorkel before breakfast. Slow farewell breakfast on the deck — passion-fruit pavlova, smoked salmon. East Air charter back to Cairns at 11am. Connect to evening international flight.

caveats: Fly-in only (East Air charter from Cairns, ~$650/person return) — book with the room.
All-inclusive at the high end: $1,500–$4,500/night per suite includes everything; activities like outer-reef trips are extra.
Cyclone season (Dec–March) — resort closes for refurbishment usually in Feb.
Phone signal limited; Wi-Fi works in the main lodge area only.
Saltwater showers in some villas (eco-design); hot fresh-water tubs available.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Among the best honeymoons in the southern hemisphere — private island, world-class snorkel from the doorstep, all-inclusive, 40 suites only. The Pavilion or a Sunset Point Villa is unforgettable.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: May–November is the peak (dry season, calm seas, manta sightings June–Aug). Avoid Dec–April cyclone risk + closure period.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: 9–12 months for The Pavilion or Sunset Point Villas in peak season. East Air charter capacity is limited — book with the room.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, but children under 10 not permitted; the resort skews heavily to couples.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: The Pavilion for the ultimate honeymoon. Sunset Point Villa for the best beach access. Anchor Bay Suite for value.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Fly to Cairns (CNS). East Air charter to Lizard Island (1h, $650 pp return). Helicopter charter from Cairns also available (~$3,500/couple).
